description:
A network performance engineer needs to profile TCP connection patterns from a packet capture to inform load balancer timeout configuration. Analyze the TCP conversations in the capture file ~/Documents/captures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng and produce two output files:

1. A text report at ~/Documents/captures/tcp_conversation_report.txt with this exact format:

TCP Conversation Analysis Report
=================================
Capture File: 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng
Total TCP Conversations: <NUMBER>
Longest Duration: <ADDR_A>:<PORT_A> <-> <ADDR_B>:<PORT_B> Duration: <SECONDS>s
Highest Volume: <ADDR_A>:<PORT_A> <-> <ADDR_B>:<PORT_B> Bytes: <TOTAL_BYTES>
Average Duration: <SECONDS>s

For 'Longest Duration', report the TCP conversation with the greatest time span (in seconds, rounded to 2 decimal places). For 'Highest Volume', report the conversation with the most total bytes transferred in both directions combined.

2. A CSV file at ~/Documents/captures/tcp_conversations.csv listing ALL TCP conversations with these columns (include a header row):
address_a,port_a,address_b,port_b,frames_a_to_b,bytes_a_to_b,frames_b_to_a,bytes_b_to_a,total_frames,total_bytes,duration_seconds

Each row in the CSV corresponds to one TCP conversation.
